methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $180M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
AMP Robotics is a Series C robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $180M+. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of nine same-sector peers at matching or adjacent stages, including eight private companies (Hadrian at $1.6B, 1X Technologies at $10.0B, Exotec at $2.0B, Bedrock Robotics at $1.8B, and Dexterity at $1.6B) and one public comparable.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $180M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.7x multiplier typical of Series C advancement, implies a valuation of $306M. Peer-median analysis across the nine-company comp set yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range spanning $300M (floor) to $10.0B (ceiling). The peer distribution reflects significant variance within the robotics sector at this stage.

Synthesizing these approaches produces a triangulated range of $260M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $953M. The gap between stage-progression and peer-median suggests meaningful uncertainty around whether AMP's profile aligns with the lower or upper end of comparable maturity. Key variables affecting valuation include the company's revenue scale relative to peers, unit economics, and addressable market penetration. The wide peer range ($300M–$10.0B) underscores sector-specific heterogeneity in funding dynamics and growth trajectories.
